title: Soca De Mayo Festival — Rum Punch Brunch
description: Soca music, Caribbean vibes, dancing, and rum punch — all afternoon. Morris Brown College hosts Atlanta's hottest pre-summer festival. Doors open 4 PM, live sets run through 11 PM. Outdoor stage, food vendors, cocktails. High-energy dancing vibe, great way to end the week and start the weekend. Bring dancing shoes.
location: Morris Brown College, 660 Campus View Ave NW, Atlanta — Sat May 2, 4 PM–11 PM
vibe: Caribbean, high-energy, festival — for couples who want to move and groove

title: WigWag Art & Music Fest — Avondale Estates
description: A full-day art and music festival just 15 min east of Atlanta. Local artists, live bands, food trucks, maker vendors. 11 AM–9 PM in the heart of Avondale Estates. Perfect for a daytime stroll through art + an evening of live music. Free to attend, food/art purchases extra. Dog-friendly. A super well-rounded date that hits art, music, and good food.
location: Washington St, Avondale Estates, GA — Sat May 2, 11 AM–9 PM
vibe: Outdoorsy, artsy, community — great for a chill daytime walk turning into live music evening

title: Atlanta Mushroom Market — Wild Heaven Beer
description: Atlanta's monthly specialty mushroom and local food market. Vendors from across the Southeast, local mushrooms, artisan goods, and Wild Heaven Beer on tap. Noon–7 PM. Bring a tote bag and graze your way through — vegetarian and local-food heaven. After, grab a pint at Wild Heaven's beautiful taproom. Free entry.
budget: Free to attend, $15–$40 for purchases + $6 pints
location: Wild Heaven Beer, 1200 Innovation Dr, Decatur — Sun May 3, 12–7 PM
vibe: Local food, community market, relaxed afternoon — perfect low-key date for foodies
